preamble: In extreme vowel reduction, an unstressed [æ] can lose both its voicing and its oral aperture, leaving only glottal frication [h]. This is a marginal process — true devoicing of [æ] typically yields a voiceless [æ̥] rather than [h], but in sufficiently reduced or whispered speech the distinction collapses toward [h].

examples.note: Marginal reduction in highly unstressed or whispered environments; more typically yields [æ̥] (voiceless vowel) rather than true [h].

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2014). A Course in Phonetics. https://archive.org/details/courseinphonetic00lade
